title: TABS an R Package
text: package provides easier way import project data Texas Architectural Barriers System database maintained Texas Department Licensing Regulation.

title: TABS an R Package
text: can install development version TABS GitHub :
code: #install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("aberuiz/TABS") #>  #> ── R CMD build ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> * checking for file ‘/private/var/folders/gh/fbq3g2jj4h9b09knhwdpmxpc0000gn/T/RtmpKW4b0y/remotes10af23cd5d4d4/aberuiz-TABS-d45f62f/DESCRIPTION’ ... OK #> * preparing ‘TABS’: #> * checking DESCRIPTION meta-information ... OK #> * checking for LF line-endings in source and make files and shell scripts #> * checking for empty or unneeded directories #> * building ‘TABS_0.1.1.tar.gz’ library(TABS)

title: TABS an R Package
text: package works primarily around one function receive projects registered TABS: GetProjects. example pull projects registered within Travis County February 29, 2024.
code: GetProjects(   county = "Travis",   reg_begin = "02/29/24",   reg_end = "03/01/24" ) #> # A tibble: 11 × 13 #>    ProjectNumber  FacilityName             City  County TypeOfWork EstimatedCost #>    <chr>          <chr>                    <chr> <chr>  <chr>              <dbl> #>  1 TABS2024012784 ATX Kitchen & Supply     Aust… Travis Renovatio…        400000 #>  2 TABS2024012779 FM 973 Retail Center     Manor Travis New Const…       2100000 #>  3 TABS2024012776 Indeed Tower             Aust… Travis Renovatio…       2224150 #>  4 TABS2024012771 South Lamar Place        Aust… Travis Renovatio…        125000 #>  5 TABS2024012741 CROSSROADS Shopping Cen… Aust… Travis Renovatio…        275000 #>  6 TABS2024012738 One Eleven Congress      Aust… Travis Renovatio…       1200000 #>  7 TABS2024012731 Round Rock Crossing      Roun… Travis Renovatio…        350000 #>  8 TABS2024012707 2300 GUADALUPE STREET    Aust… Travis Renovatio…        375000 #>  9 TABS2024012692 Austin Community Colleg… Aust… Travis Renovatio…        100000 #> 10 TABS2024012676 AMD                      Aust… Travis Renovatio…       1000000 #> 11 TABS2024012665 Bee Cave Shopping Center Bee … Travis Renovatio…         90000 #> # ℹ 7 more variables: ProjectStatus <chr>, ProjectId <chr>, ProjectName <chr>, #> #   ProjectCreatedOn <chr>, DataVersionId <int>, EstimatedStartDate <chr>, #> #   EstimatedEndDate <chr>

title: TABS an R Package
text: addition dates can search projects county, city, address, owner name, facility, /project name. example search projects City Austin owned Tesla.
code: GetProjects(   city = "Austin",   owner = "Tesla" ) #> # A tibble: 47 × 13 #>    ProjectNumber  FacilityName            City   County TypeOfWork EstimatedCost #>    <chr>          <chr>                   <chr>  <chr>  <chr>              <dbl> #>  1 TABS2026006064 Tesla                   Austin Travis New Const…        250000 #>  2 TABS2025021701 Tesla Gigafactory       Austin Travis New Const…         50000 #>  3 TABS2025021600 Tesla Gigafactory       Austin Travis New Const…         50000 #>  4 TABS2025020452 Tesla Gigafactory       Austin Travis New Const…         50000 #>  5 TABS2025020451 Tesla Gigafactory       Austin Travis New Const…         50000 #>  6 TABS2025020450 Tesla Gigafactory       Austin Travis New Const…         50000 #>  7 TABS2025020449 Tesla Gigafactory       Austin Travis New Const…         50000 #>  8 TABS2025020448 Tesla Gigafactory       Austin Travis New Const…         50000 #>  9 TABS2025011400 Tesla Gigafactory Texas Austin Travis New Const…         50000 #> 10 TABS2025008524 Tesla                   Austin Travis New Const…        200000 #> # ℹ 37 more rows #> # ℹ 7 more variables: ProjectStatus <chr>, ProjectId <chr>, ProjectName <chr>, #> #   ProjectCreatedOn <chr>, DataVersionId <int>, EstimatedStartDate <chr>, #> #   EstimatedEndDate <chr>

title: TABS an R Package
text: Registration Dates: Dates required mm/dd/yy format. automating dates, can use format(Sys.Date(), "%D"). Date arguments passed database midnight therefore, reg_begin 02/29/24 include projects starting February 29, 2024 reg_end 03/01/24 mean projects started 03/01/24 later included. Name Matching City county names matched case-insensitively, surrounding whitespace ignored, capitalization longer needs match. Spelling internal spacing must still match TDLR lists name (example, "De Witt", "Dewitt").

title: Get registered projects — GetProjects
text: Returns df projects registered TABS database

title: Get registered projects — GetProjects
code: GetProjects(   county = NULL,   city = NULL,   reg_begin = "",   reg_end = "",   owner = "",   project = "",   facility = "",   address = "" )

title: Get registered projects — GetProjects
text: county Texas county physical project city Texas city physical project reg_begin start date searching projects Date Format: mm/dd/yy reg_end cutoff date searching projects Date Format: mm/dd/yy owner Search owner project project Search project name facility Search facility name address Search physical address project

title: Get registered projects — GetProjects
code: GetProjects(   county = "Caldwell",   reg_begin = "03/01/24",   reg_end = "04/01/24" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 13 #>   ProjectNumber FacilityName City  County TypeOfWork EstimatedCost ProjectStatus #>   <chr>         <chr>        <chr> <chr>  <chr>              <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 TABS20240143… City of Loc… Lock… Caldw… Renovatio…        878720 Review Compl… #> 2 TABS20240138… Main Church… Dale  Caldw… New Const…        225000 Review Compl… #> # ℹ 6 more variables: ProjectId <chr>, ProjectName <chr>, #> #   ProjectCreatedOn <chr>, DataVersionId <int>, EstimatedStartDate <chr>, #> #   EstimatedEndDate <chr> GetProjects(   city = "Austin",   owner = "Tesla",   reg_begin = "01/01/20",   reg_end = "01/01/24" ) #> # A tibble: 18 × 13 #>    ProjectNumber  FacilityName        City   County TypeOfWork     EstimatedCost #>    <chr>          <chr>               <chr>  <chr>  <chr>                  <dbl> #>  1 TABS2024005095 "Tesla"             Austin Travis New Construct…        180000 #>  2 TABS2024005089 "Tesla"             Austin Travis New Construct…        170000 #>  3 TABS2024005082 "Tesla"             Austin Travis New Construct…        170000 #>  4 TABS2024001696 "Shop: GAX"         Austin Travis Renovation/Al…      10500000 #>  5 TABS2024001561 "Tesla"             Austin Travis New Construct…        180000 #>  6 TABS2023022484 "Tesla"             Austin Travis New Construct…        170000 #>  7 TABS2023014558 "Tesla, Inc."       Austin Travis New Construct…        140000 #>  8 TABS2023009082 "Die Shop"          Austin Travis New Construct…      59000000 #>  9 TABS2023008989 "Cell 1"            Austin Travis New Construct…     368000000 #> 10 TABS2023008982 "Drive Unit"        Austin Travis New Construct…      85000000 #> 11 TABS2023008949 "Cathode"           Austin Travis New Construct…     260000000 #> 12 TABS2023008944 "Cell Test Lab"     Austin Travis New Construct…       3700000 #> 13 TABS2023007945 "Plastics"          Austin Travis New Construct…      58000000 #> 14 TABS2022005792 "Body in White"     Austin Travis New Construct…     182000000 #> 15 TABS2022005791 "Stamping "         Austin Travis New Construct…     150000000 #> 16 TABS2022005790 "Casting"           Austin Travis New Construct…     109000000 #> 17 TABS2022005789 "Paint"             Austin Travis New Construct…     126000000 #> 18 TABS2022005788 "General Assembly " Austin Travis New Construct…     493000000 #> # ℹ 7 more variables: ProjectStatus <chr>, ProjectId <chr>, ProjectName <chr>, #> #   ProjectCreatedOn <chr>, DataVersionId <int>, EstimatedStartDate <chr>, #> #   EstimatedEndDate <chr>

what: Recode values to and from TDLR — TABSdecoder
title: Recode values to and from TDLR — TABSdecoder
text: submit appropriate codes TDLR return plain language values user. Name lookups case-insensitive ignore surrounding whitespace.

title: Recode values to and from TDLR — TABSdecoder
Recode values to and from TDLR — TABSdecoder
text: codes variable sent codebook deciphering codebook_df codebook dataframe check codes , default uses built `codebook`

title: Codebook — codebook
text: Table decoding numerical codes descriptive values TDLR

title: Codebook — codebook
text: data frame 2 variables: code Numerical code value Descriptive value/label

title: TABS 0.1.2
text: TABSdecoder() — therefore GetProjects() — now matches city county names case-insensitively ignores surrounding whitespace, capitalization longer needs match TDLR exactly. Refreshed codebook TDLR. County 2062 now "De Witt" (previously "Dewitt"); note internal spacing must matched.
